alternatives.name: Restaurant365 OData connector
alternatives.summary: Exposes the raw reporting views for BI and warehouse tools.

alternatives.name: General OData clients
alternatives.summary: Can fetch rows but do not encode Restaurant365-specific refresh, redaction, and tombstone workflows.

gaps: Restaurant365 OData is useful for warehouses, but raw OData clients do not explain which views should be rowVersion incremental, date-window refreshed, or full refreshed.
Private restaurant tenants need validation commands that prove access without printing row values.
Delete handling requires EntityDeleted tombstones, which are easy to miss in a generic view list.
patterns: Use metadata first: list views and field names before fetching rows.
Keep tenant validation redacted by default.
Use DateTimeOffset boundaries for Restaurant365 date filters.
Prefer rowVersion watermarks where Restaurant365 exposes them.

novel_features.name: Redacted tenant sampler
novel_features.description: Samples one Restaurant365 OData view while returning only columns and row counts by default.
novel_features.rationale: Engineers often need to prove a tenant is reachable without exposing restaurant names, IDs, amounts, or employee data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for safe access validation in private Restaurant365 tenants.

novel_features.name: Refresh planner
novel_features.description: Builds Restaurant365-safe date-window and rowVersion request plans without fetching rows.
novel_features.rationale: Warehouse jobs need bounded daily or monthly slices for sales, labor, and payroll views, plus rowVersion continuation for transaction views.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before export or sync so large refreshes become explicit, reviewable request chunks.

novel_features.name: Delete tombstone counter
novel_features.description: Reads EntityDeleted with optional entity and rowVersion filters, returning counts by default.
novel_features.rationale: Incremental pipelines need delete handling, but tombstone rows can contain private identifiers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when checking whether an incremental load must process deletes.

novel_features.name: Metadata-first discovery
novel_features.description: Uses $metadata to report supported views, field counts, field types, date fields, rowVersion support, and refresh pattern.
novel_features.rationale: Restaurant365 deployments vary by tenant and product modules, so engineers need schema checks before pulls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before mapping fields into a warehouse model.

novel_features.name: Bounded export guard
novel_features.description: Exports a view to JSONL or CSV and requires date filters for known date-window views.
novel_features.rationale: Date-backed Restaurant365 views can be large; accidental broad pulls are slow and risky.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for explicit slices that fit daily refresh and backfill jobs.

narrative.headline: A read-only Restaurant365 OData CLI for warehouse refresh planning and safe tenant validation.
narrative.value_prop: Restaurant365 OData exposes useful reporting views, but data engineers still need schema discovery, redacted validation, bounded backfills, rowVersion planning, and delete-tombstone checks before loading a warehouse.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set RESTAURANT365_ODATA_USERNAME and RESTAURANT365_ODATA_PASSWORD for HTTP Basic authentication. The short R365_ODATA_* aliases are also supported.

quickstart.comment: Inspect views and refresh patterns without fetching row values.
Validate a tenant with redacted output.

troubleshoots.symptom: A date filter returns a type coercion error.
troubleshoots.fix: Use DateTimeOffset boundaries such as 2026-05-01T00:00:00Z instead of bare dates.

troubleshoots.symptom: A schema field count is missing.
troubleshoots.fix: Run describe-view and check the tenant's $metadata; view names can differ in casing.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for read-only Restaurant365 OData discovery, validation, refresh planning, export, sync, and delete-tombstone checks. Do not use it for custom customer APIs or tenant-specific private endpoints.

recipes.title: Plan a sales backfill
recipes.explanation: Returns bounded DateTimeOffset filter chunks without fetching rows.

recipes.title: Continue a transaction incremental load
recipes.explanation: Returns a rowVersion filter for the next incremental pull.
